name: First Classical Pieces for Kids
description: A friendly starter playlist of recognizable, colorful classical pieces that are easy for young children to enjoy.
pieces: Dance of the Sugar Plum Fairy
parent_note: Start here if a child is new to classical music. These pieces are short, memorable, and easy to connect with stories or movement.
kid_prompt: Can you hear the birds, the dancing fairy, or the singing cello?

name: Bedtime Classical Calm
description: Soft, gentle music for winding down before sleep.
parent_note: Use at low volume during bedtime routines, nursing, reading, or quiet cuddling.
kid_prompt: Which piece sounds the sleepiest?

name: Magical Classical Music
description: Sparkling, mysterious, and fairy-tale-like music for imaginative listening.
pieces: Dance of the Sugar Plum Fairy
parent_note: Great for pretend play, drawing, fairy-tale themes, or holiday programming.
kid_prompt: What kind of magical place does this music make you imagine?

name: Big Adventure Music
description: Bold and exciting music that feels like a journey, chase, or heroic story.
parent_note: Good for movement games, dramatic play, or introducing orchestral power.
kid_prompt: Does this sound like flying, running, sailing, or saving the day?

name: Silly and Playful Classical
description: Funny, bouncy, surprising pieces that help kids hear humor in classical music.
pieces: In the Hall of the Mountain King
parent_note: Use this playlist to show that classical music is not always serious. It can be funny, surprising, and full of character.
kid_prompt: Which part sounds the funniest?

name: Classical Music for Dancing
description: Elegant, rhythmic, and energetic pieces that invite children to move.
parent_note: Encourage kids to sway, march, spin, clap, or make up their own dances.
kid_prompt: Can you march, spin, or tiptoe to this music?

name: Nature in Classical Music
description: Pieces that evoke birds, seasons, animals, water, and outdoor scenes.
parent_note: Great for connecting classical music to the natural world and helping kids visualize sound.
kid_prompt: What animal, weather, or season do you hear?

name: Quiet Drawing Music
description: Gentle, flowing music that supports creative focus without being too distracting.
parent_note: Useful during art time, coloring, building, or quiet independent play.
kid_prompt: What colors would you use for this music?

name: Emotional Classical Moments
description: Beautiful, expressive pieces that help children notice feelings in music.
parent_note: Use this playlist to talk about how music can feel sad, tender, hopeful, or powerful.
kid_prompt: Does this music feel happy, sad, calm, or brave?

name: Classical Music for Morning
description: Bright and uplifting pieces to start the day with energy and positivity.
parent_note: Good for breakfast, getting dressed, or a cheerful drive to school.
kid_prompt: Which piece makes you feel most awake?

name: Spooky Classical Music
description: Dark, mysterious, and dramatic pieces for Halloween or pretend spooky stories.
pieces: Toccata and Fugue in D minor
In the Hall of the Mountain King
parent_note: Best for older kids who enjoy spooky stories, dramatic sound effects, and Halloween themes.
kid_prompt: Does this sound like a castle, a monster, a storm, or a chase?

name: Holiday Classical Favorites
description: Warm, festive, and magical music for winter holidays and family gatherings.
pieces: Dance of the Sugar Plum Fairy
parent_note: Good for holiday mornings, family gatherings, or Nutcracker-themed listening.
kid_prompt: Which one sounds most like winter magic?

name: Learn the String Family
description: A playlist highlighting violin, viola, cello, and full string ensemble sounds.
parent_note: Use this playlist to help kids hear how string instruments can sing, dance, sparkle, and support each other.
kid_prompt: Can you hear the violin, cello, or all the strings together?

name: Learn the Orchestra
description: A guided playlist showing different sections of the orchestra and how they create color.
parent_note: Good for teaching instrument families: strings, woodwinds, brass, percussion, and keyboard colors.
kid_prompt: Which instrument do you notice first?

name: Classical Music for Car Rides
description: Balanced, familiar, and engaging music that works well while driving.
parent_note: A mix of classical and familiar orchestral favorites to keep kids engaged without overwhelming them.
kid_prompt: Which piece feels like moving down the road?

name: Baby's First Classical Playlist
description: A very gentle introduction to classical music for babies and toddlers.
parent_note: Keep volume low and use during calm routines. Avoid overly dramatic pieces for this age group.
kid_prompt: Can you rock slowly with the music?

name: Popsical Concert Warm-Up
description: A family-friendly playlist to help children get excited before attending a live classical concert.
pieces: Dance of the Sugar Plum Fairy
parent_note: Use before a Popsical-style concert so kids recognize sounds, instruments, and musical moods when they arrive.
kid_prompt: Which instrument do you want to see in person?

name: Tiny Dancer Classical Playlist
description: Short, danceable pieces for toddlers and young children who want to move.
parent_note: Encourage simple movements: clapping, marching, spinning, tiptoeing, and freezing when the music stops.
kid_prompt: Can you make your body match the music?

name: Calm After Big Feelings
description: Music for helping children transition from overstimulation, tantrums, or big emotions into calm.
parent_note: Use as background while breathing, cuddling, reading, or quietly resetting after high energy.
kid_prompt: Can you take one slow breath with the music?

name: Classical Music That Sounds Like Animals
description: Animal-inspired classical pieces that help children connect sound with imagination.
parent_note: Ask children to guess the animal or character before telling them the title.
kid_prompt: What animal do you hear?

name: Powerful Orchestra Moments
description: Big, bold orchestral pieces that show the excitement of classical music.
parent_note: This playlist is more intense, so it works best for kids who enjoy big sounds and drama.
kid_prompt: Which piece sounds the strongest?

name: Classical Music for Storytime
description: Music that supports stories, imagination, and pretend play.
pieces: In the Hall of the Mountain King
parent_note: Pause between pieces and ask kids what happened in the story they imagined.
kid_prompt: What happened at the beginning, middle, and end?

name: Singable Classics and Familiar Tunes
description: Classical and orchestral favorites with melodies children can hum or sing.
pieces: Can You Feel the Love Tonight
parent_note: Useful for families who want a bridge between familiar songs and classical listening.
kid_prompt: Can you hum the melody after you hear it?

name: Gentle Cello and Strings
description: Warm, expressive string music centered around cello and lyrical string textures.
parent_note: A strong playlist for showing how string instruments can sound like singing voices.
kid_prompt: Does the cello sound like a person singing?

name: Fast and Exciting Classical Music
description: Short, high-energy pieces with strong rhythm, speed, and momentum.
pieces: Overture to The Marriage of Figaro
parent_note: Good for active listening, dancing, or comparing fast and slow music.
kid_prompt: Which piece is the fastest?

name: Peaceful Piano for Kids
description: Soft piano pieces that introduce children to calm solo piano music.
parent_note: Great for showing that one instrument can create a full emotional world.
kid_prompt: What picture does the piano make in your mind?
